label: dissertation_v4_orca_fusion_m10_ultra_quiet_na013
description: Active dissertation screening defaults for the Hamamatsu ORCA-Fusion C14440-20UP at the M=10 design magnification. The ideal 166Er atomic Faraday coefficient is fixed analytically; the effective NA=0.130 optical design and catalogue-typical detector values remain commissioning inputs until measured on the apparatus.

imaging_geometry.imaging_axis_note: axis 0 is the across-cigar x direction; the plotted transverse plane is y,z.
imaging_geometry.focal_length_1_role: effective object-side focal length of the active NA=0.130 design; with the 24 mm entrance pupil it gives NA=(D/2)/f=0.130
imaging_geometry.focal_length_2_role: legacy relay entry retained for optical-model compatibility; it is not used to infer the active camera magnification
imaging_geometry.magnification_status: current experimental design value; achieved magnification remains to be measured during commissioning
imaging_geometry.camera_pixel_role: catalogue physical sensor pitch; code divides by magnification exactly once

faraday_recovery.kappa_F_status: ideal atomic-response coefficient for the fully spin-polarised 166Er reference state probed along the quantisation axis; apparatus-level response remains to be calibrated

camera_recovery.sampling_note: The optical grid remains 100 um/1024. A centred 153x153 analysis crop is integrated at the exact 0.650 um object-plane pitch; it is not claimed to be a directly programmed hardware ROI. ORCA-Fusion sub-array dimensions are set in multiples of four pixels, so commissioning must acquire a legal ROI containing both analyser ports before applying this crop.
camera_recovery.camera_exposure_role: shortest calculated Ultra quiet exposure setting that contains the 300 us optical pulse at the F=300 mW us, P=1 mW reference; tau in F=P tau remains the optical pulse duration and is placed inside a verified common-exposure interval
camera_recovery.quantum_efficiency_note: catalogue typical QE at 400 nm, used provisionally for the 401 nm transition until effective in-path response is measured
camera_recovery.read_noise_note: manufacturer-typical rms value for Ultra quiet scan; to be verified with dark frames for the installed camera, ROI and trigger sequence
camera_recovery.read_noise_treatment: use 0.7 e- rms per physical pixel and readout in the active low-noise dissertation screening scenario
camera_recovery.pulse_timing_requirement: use a global-reset trigger or place the probe pulse inside a verified common-exposure interval shared by every row of both analyser-port regions
camera_recovery.dual_port_sampling: both Wollaston outputs are represented as simultaneous regions of interest on one sensor; port separation and the common exposure timing remain to be measured

multishot_recovery.recoil_energy_convention: complete absorption-spontaneous-emission cycle deposits 2 E_rec per primary or reabsorbed scattering event

noisy_multishot_filmstrip_recovery.profile_convention: fresh-cloud image formation with the evolving frame state supplied by the full multishot model

status_note: Active ORCA-Fusion/M=10 low-noise screening configuration. The ideal atomic coefficient is kappa_F=-45/91. Effective NA=0.130 is an optical design assumption, while QE=0.65 and Ultra quiet read noise=0.7 e- rms are manufacturer-typical inputs that require apparatus verification.
